text: International Media Cultural References (IMCR) recognized the different existing media and content diversity. An in-depth analysis of entertainment with a heatmap and different statistical dissection of media was presented by Observable Plot [1]. IMCR though was composed of an analysis from the Wikipedia (Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License (“CC BY-SA 4.0”), GNU Free Documentation License (“GFDL”) (unversioned, with no invariant sections, front-cover texts, or back-cover texts)) [2-5] time information: year, month, day number (odd or even) and time of day (hour) was from individual information of 1388 significant media [2-1393]. IMCR combined HTML, CSS (CSS Grid), javascript, data technologies [1, 1394-1396] and with additional non-data aspect technologies [1397-1400]. Additionally, adapted the description by Observable Plot of asynchronous javascript [1], then it was further adapted and modified for asynchronous javascript with IMCR information [1]. An adaptation of licenses: for visualization of all licenses [1401], for license retention of the data aspect [2-1393] and all technologies [1, 1394-1400, details of licenses: 1402]. IMCR was licensed as The Unlicense [1402, 1403] only for the HTML, CSS (CSS Grid) of IMCR, javascript and not all the other technologies [1, 1394-1400] or the data aspect [2-1393].
textp2: The representation of data technology for Figure 1, 4-5 was with Observable's D3 (ISC) [1394]. Figure 1 was an adapted and modified Radial dendogram, Radial cluster tree [1394]. Modifications: const cx = width * 0.53; const cy = height * 0.51; const radius = Math.min(width, height) / 2 - 90; the .attr with “style”, “width”, “height”, “font” of const svg. and info1.append(svg.node()); where info1 as id and adapted append modification [1394]. Adapted asynchronous javascript [1].
textp3: Figure 4 was an adapted and modified Marimekko [1394]. Modifications: const width = 65000; const marginTop = 1000; const marginRight = 11500; const marginBottom = -5050; const marginLeft = 100; the .attr with “style”, “width”, “max-width”, “height”, “font” of const svg. where modification was the font.  const color = d3.scaleOrdinal(d3.schemeCategory10).domain(data.map(d => d.month)); modification of the data.map and .month, (d3.hierarchy(d3.group(data, d => d.year, d => d.month)).sum(d => d.value)) where modification was d.year, d.month, const root = treemap(data); where modification was data. info4.append(svg.node()); where info4 was the id and adapted append modification [1394]. Modification for JSON [1394] and adapted asynchronous javascript [1].
textp4: Figure 5 was adapted and modified Zoomable Sunburst [1394]. Modifications: const width = 528; info5.append(svg.node()); where info5 was the id and an adapted append modification [1394]. Adapted asynchronous javascript [1].
textp5: PlotlyJS (MIT) was used for Figure 2 and 6 [1395-1396]. For Figure 2, adapted and modified “Control Text Orientation Inside Pie Chart Sectors” [1395-1396]. Modifications: height, width, const, info2 as id. Adapted asynchronous javascript [1]. Figure 6 had an adaptation and modification of “Rounded Corners on Bars” [1395-1396]. Modifications: no rounded corners, const, and info6 as id. Adapted asynchronous javascript [1].
textp6: Figure 3 was an adapted and modified “Vertical bar chart” with Observable's Plot (ISC) [1]. Modifications: Plot.barY with an exclusion of the reverse. Also, percent was eliminated from the y object. There was another append adaptation and info3 was id [1]. Adapted and modified “Vertical bars, rotated labels” for JSON input with another modified included adapted asynchronous javascript [1]. Adapted and modified for inclusion of a different title [1].

text1: Figure 1, Media Titles in Radial Dendrogram, Radial Cluster Tree

text2: Figure 1 contains 1388 titles to the Wikipedia year and month (Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License (“CC BY-SA 4.0”), GNU Free Documentation License (“GFDL”) (unversioned, with no invariant sections, front-cover texts, or back-cover texts)) [2-1393].

text3: Figure 2, Pie Chart of Total Media Information Per Year

text4: Figure 2 is the percentage proportion of 1388 media from Wikipedia (Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License (“CC BY-SA 4.0”), GNU Free Documentation License (“GFDL”) (unversioned, with no invariant sections, front-cover texts, or back-cover texts)) year [2-1393]. 2026 had the highest percentage (80.3%), followed by 2025 (18.1%), 2024 (1.15%) and 2023 (0.504%).

text5: Figure 3, Bar Chart of Total Media Information Per Month

text6: Adapted from plot gallery and modified to lead to highest total month [1]. The most total included May, April and March from figure 3 of the media in months Wikipedia (Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License (“CC BY-SA 4.0”), GNU Free Documentation License (“GFDL”) (unversioned, with no invariant sections, front-cover texts, or back-cover texts)) [2-1393].

text7: Figure 4, Marimekko of Total Media Information Per Month Each Year

text8: The distribution of media Wikipedia (Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License (“CC BY-SA 4.0”), GNU Free Documentation License (“GFDL”) (unversioned, with no invariant sections, front-cover texts, or back-cover texts)) months in 2023-2026 from Figure 4 [2-1393].

text9: Figure 5, Total Media Information Per Year, Month and Odd Or Even Number Day in Zoomable Sunburst

text10: Figure 5 is the 1388 media Wikipedia (Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License (“CC BY-SA 4.0”), GNU Free Documentation License (“GFDL”) (unversioned, with no invariant sections, front-cover texts, or back-cover texts) in year, then month, then even or odd numbered days [2-1393].

text11: Figure 6, Bar Chart of 24 Hours Distribution Total Media Information

text12: From Figure 6, there was more totals of media Wikipedia (Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License (“CC BY-SA 4.0”), GNU Free Documentation License (“GFDL”) (unversioned, with no invariant sections, front-cover texts, or back-cover texts)) 24 hr time in year 2026 compared with all other years [2-1393].
